deep_dive.title: Empowering Polymeric Materials Discovery by Artificial Intelligence
deep_dive.abstract: Polymeric materials underpin modern technologies spanning energy storage, microelectronics, healthcare and sustainable manufacturing. Yet their rational design remains exceptionally challenging because material performance emerges from complex interactions among molecular composition, chain architecture, processing history and hierarchical structural evolution across multiple length and time scales. Consequently, polymer research has long relied on labor-intensive experimentation and fragmented modeling approaches, limiting both mechanistic understanding and innovation efficiency. Recent advances in data infrastructure, machine learning, large artificial intelligence (AI) models and laboratory automation are beginning to reshape this landscape. Rather than functioning as isolated tools, polymer databases, predictive models, AI agents and automated laboratories are increasingly converging into interconnected discovery ecosystems. As a result, the central challenge is shifting from improving predictive accuracy alone to enabling reliable decision-making, adaptive learning and seamless integration across computation, experimentation and scientific reasoning. We argue that polymer science is entering an era of autonomous discovery, in which data, simulation, reasoning and experimentation operate within self-improving feedback loops that continuously generate hypotheses, design materials, execute experiments and refine predictive models. By unifying molecular design, process optimization, experimental validation and industrial translation, such autonomous ecosystems establish a more predictive, reproducible and scalable paradigm for polymer innovation, fundamentally transforming how polymer research is conducted.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: AI-driven discovery of polymeric materials, directly bridging computation and synthesis in polymer science.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: AI驱动的高分子材料发现，直接连接计算与合成。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: AI驱动的高分子材料发现，直接连接计算与合成。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: AI驱动的高分子材料发现，直接连接计算与合成。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This review highlights recent advances in AI, machine learning, and automation that are transforming polymeric materials discovery by addressing the complexity of composition-structure-property relationships.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 高分子材料设计因组分、链结构、加工历史等多尺度复杂相互作用而极具挑战。本文评述了数据基础设施、机器学习、大型AI模型和实验室自动化的最新进展，它们正重塑聚合物研究范式。这些技术能够加速机理理解与创新效率，为计算辅助有机合成与高分子交叉领域提供关键方法和工具，推动功能高分子材料的理性设计。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 计算辅助有机合成与高分子/功能低聚物交叉领域的顶刊文献检索与设计思路解析, especially work that connects or combines: Computationally-Assisted Organic Synthesis; density functional theory mechanistic study providing causal explanation for experimental observations; computational virtual screening of reaction conditions and catalyst scaffolds; Organic Synthesis.

quick_skim.title: Target-Aware Bandit Allocation for Scalable Surrogate Optimization in Chemical Space
quick_skim.abstract: Identifying high-utility candidates from massive discrete spaces under expensive evaluations is a recurring challenge across the sciences, with structure-based drug discovery as a prominent example. While surrogate-based optimization can increase sample efficiency by reducing the number of expensive evaluations, modern molecular libraries have reached billions to trillions of compounds, making full-library surrogate inference itself a major computational bottleneck. We introduce BOBa, a bandit-guided surrogate optimization framework that eliminates full-library inference by adaptively allocating computation across partitions of the action space. By treating partitions as arms in a multi-armed bandit, BOBa concentrates inference and evaluations on empirically promising partitions while maintaining principled exploration. Experiments on real-world synthesis-on-demand libraries demonstrate that optimism-under-uncertainty bandits, combined with meaningful action space partitioning, are essential for effective allocation of inference and evaluations. Our findings reveal a tunable tradeoff between screening performance and surrogate inference cost, which supports practical optimization over current libraries, and establishes a viable route to ultra-large library virtual screening.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: bandit-guided surrogate optimization for efficient virtual screening in massive chemical spaces
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多臂老虎机引导的替代优化，用于大规模化学空间的高效虚拟筛选
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多臂老虎机引导的替代优化，用于大规模化学空间的高效虚拟筛选
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多臂老虎机引导的替代优化，用于大规模化学空间的高效虚拟筛选
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces BOBa, a bandit-guided surrogate optimization framework that efficiently identifies high-utility candidates from billion-scale molecular libraries without full-library inference, applicable to catalyst and reaction condition screening.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该工作针对数十亿级分子库的候选筛选计算瓶颈，提出BOBa框架，通过将化学空间划分为子集并采用多臂老虎机自适应分配推理资源，实现无需全库打分的高效替代优化，可应用于催化剂和反应条件的虚拟筛选，显著提升计算辅助合成中大规模探索的效率。

quick_skim.title: Kinetics of template-directed multistate copolymerization
quick_skim.abstract: We consider processes of template-directed multistate copolymerization by molecular machines such as polymerases or ribosomes, having multiple states of conformation or activation. We show that the kinetic equations of these processes can be exactly solved for the mean growth velocity, the sequence probabilities of the grown copy, and the local probabilities and fractions of monomeric units in the copy. Asymptotically, in the long-time limit, the kinetic equations are solved with a matrix factorization ansatz in terms of a backward iteration, forming an iterated matrix function system, and a complementary forward iteration, both running along the template sequence. The iterative method is very significantly faster than usual computational methods, as demonstrated with a numerical example.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Computational modeling of copolymerization kinetics relevant to polymer synthesis theme.

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Exact solution of kinetic equations for template-directed multistate copolymerization using a matrix factorization ansatz, providing fast computation of growth velocity and sequence probabilities.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对模板指导的多态共聚合过程，提出了一种基于矩阵分解的精确求解方法，可快速计算平均生长速度、序列概率等。该方法利用向后迭代的矩阵函数系统，比常规计算方法显著加速。这为高分子序列的精准模拟提供了理论工具，可应用于功能低聚物设计。

quick_skim.title: Uncertainty-aware reinforcement learning for chemical language models
quick_skim.abstract: Reinforcement Learning (RL) has become a powerful paradigm for de novo molecular design, enabling Chemical Language Models (CLMs) to navigate and explore the chemical space while optimizing specific desired properties. However, the existing RL frameworks treat all scoring functions as deterministic oracles, neglecting the inherent uncertainty attached to the predictions of the different molecular properties. This can lead to the exploration of highly-uncertain regions of the chemical space, focusing on the generation of highly scored molecules which are poorly supported by the training data. This can destabilize the optimization process, yielding predictions that are far from their true values.   We propose and compare two complementary ways of incorporating predictive uncertainty into RL. In the first one, uncertainty is treated as an additional optimization objective and incorporated along with the rest of the scoring functions, allowing the policy to trade off exploitation against reliability. Secondly, uncertainty is used to modulate policy updates, reducing the influence of molecules whose properties lie far outside the scoring function confidence domain.   Both approaches were evaluated across three different settings: (i) a controlled model system, in which the prediction error is modeled as a Gaussian distribution, with a variance proportional to the distance to the training data; and two real-world tasks, making use of (ii) ChemProp models and (iii) a Conformal Prediction wrapper applied to a Random forest classifier.   We show that uncertainty-aware RL enables CLMs to explore chemical space more robustly by favoring lower-uncertainty regions. This leads to more reliable hit discovery without compromising molecular score, increasing the true hit rate by 0.25 (from 0.5 to 0.75), and nearly doubling the total number of true hits.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Uncertainty-aware RL improves de novo molecular design, supporting computationally-assisted synthesis.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 不确定性感知的强化学习改进从头分子设计，支持计算辅助合成。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 不确定性感知的强化学习改进从头分子设计，支持计算辅助合成。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 不确定性感知的强化学习改进从头分子设计，支持计算辅助合成。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: The paper introduces uncertainty estimation in RL for chemical language models, preventing over-exploitation of unreliable molecular property predictions and stabilizing optimization.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 强化学习用于分子设计时常将评分函数视为确定性预言机，忽略预测不确定性，导致探索不可靠区域。本文提出两种互补方式将不确定性纳入RL过程，比较其在分子优化中的效果。该方法能引导模型远离数据稀疏区，提高生成分子的可靠性，为计算辅助有机合成中的虚拟筛选和分子设计提供更稳健的策略。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 强化学习用于分子设计时常将评分函数视为确定性预言机，忽略预测不确定性，导致探索不可靠区域。本文提出两种互补方式将不确定性纳入RL过程，比较其在分子优化中的效果。该方法能引导模型远离数据稀疏区，提高生成分子的可靠性，为计算辅助有机合成中的虚拟筛选和分子设计提供更稳健的策略。

quick_skim.title: Budget-Constrained Compound Library Prioritization with Risk Awareness and Uncertainty Quantification
quick_skim.abstract: Early discovery projects often face a budgeted prioritization problem: many structures can be enumerated or purchased, but only a small fraction can be tested, reviewed, or synthesized first. I formulate this setting as risk-aware compound-library compression. Given a molecular library and a fixed Top-k budget, the goal is to return an enriched candidate subset while preserving uncertainty, applicability-domain evidence, ADMET/structural alerts, and audit fields needed for human review. The framework intentionally uses a transparent 2D activity proxy rather than a complex representation model, combining Morgan fingerprints, RDKit descriptors, a multilayer perceptron, split-conformal uncertainty intervals, leakage auditing, and auditable export. On ChEMBL 36, the model achieved Spearman 0.7674 and EF@1% 2.7331 on internal validation, and Spearman 0.5171 with EF@1% 2.4359 on a temporal holdout. After fold-0 training-overlap control, a scaffold-disjoint BACE subset retained ROC AUC 0.7626 and EF@1% 2.0253. In a strict 100-molecule BACE decision-layer replay, risk-aware ordering kept Hit@10 at 0.9000 while exposing review evidence that pure activity sorting omits. An EGFR/CHEMBL203 label-hidden operational replay supports workflow feasibility but is reported as same-source sensitivity analysis rather than independent external validation. The claim is bounded: the evidence supports risk-aware library compression as an upstream prioritization layer, while prospective blinded validation remains necessary before claiming project-specific hit-rate or cost improvements.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: budget-constrained compound library prioritization to decide which molecules to synthesize first
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 预算约束下化合物库优先级排序，决定优先合成哪些分子
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 预算约束下化合物库优先级排序，决定优先合成哪些分子
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 预算约束下化合物库优先级排序，决定优先合成哪些分子
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a risk-aware compound-library compression method for budgeted prioritization, directly aiding decisions on which molecules to synthesize in early discovery.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对早期药物发现中需在有限预算下优先选择分子进行合成与测试的问题，提出风险感知的化合物库压缩方法，结合2D活性代理模型和保形不确定性量化，为合成决策提供透明可信的支持，但未涉及反应条件或催化剂筛选，属于计算辅助合成决策的工具性工作。
